title: Subject Performance for Student
description: Displays a single student's score across every subject they have been graded in, with one bar per subject. Useful for identifying individual strengths and weaknesses.
sql_query: SELECT s.name AS subject_name, g.score AS score FROM grades g JOIN subjects s ON g.subject_id = s.id WHERE g.student_id = :student_id ORDER BY s.name

title: Average Score by Subject
description: Shows the average percentage score achieved by all students for each subject. Highlights which subjects students tend to perform best or worst in overall.
sql_query: SELECT s.name AS subject_name, ROUND(AVG(g.score / g.max_score * 100), 2) AS avg_score_percent FROM grades g JOIN subjects s ON g.subject_id = s.id GROUP BY s.id, s.name ORDER BY avg_score_percent DESC LIMIT 50

title: Subject Completion Status Breakdown
description: Breaks down all student-subject enrolments by their current completion status (not started, in progress, completed). Gives a quick overview of overall course progress across the institution.
sql_query: SELECT ssc.status AS completion_status, COUNT(*) AS student_count FROM student_subject_completion ssc GROUP BY ssc.status ORDER BY student_count DESC

title: Completion Rate by Subject
description: Shows the percentage of enrolled students who have fully completed each subject. Helps identify subjects with low completion rates that may need curriculum or support improvements.
sql_query: SELECT s.name AS subject_name, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN ssc.status = 'completed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) * 100, 2) AS completion_rate_percent FROM student_subject_completion ssc JOIN subjects s ON ssc.subject_id = s.id GROUP BY s.id, s.name ORDER BY completion_rate_percent DESC LIMIT 50

title: Average Score by Subject Complexity
description: Compares the average student score across subjects grouped by complexity level (easy, medium, hard). Reveals whether subject difficulty correlates with lower academic performance.
sql_query: SELECT s.complexity AS complexity_level, ROUND(AVG(g.score / g.max_score * 100), 2) AS avg_score_percent FROM grades g JOIN subjects s ON g.subject_id = s.id GROUP BY s.complexity ORDER BY FIELD(s.complexity, 'easy', 'medium', 'hard')

title: Average Time Taken to Complete Each Subject
description: Displays the average number of hours students spent on each subject before completing it. Helps identify subjects that require the most student effort.
sql_query: SELECT s.name AS subject_name, ROUND(AVG(ssc.time_taken_hours), 2) AS avg_hours FROM student_subject_completion ssc JOIN subjects s ON ssc.subject_id = s.id WHERE ssc.time_taken_hours IS NOT NULL GROUP BY s.id, s.name ORDER BY avg_hours DESC LIMIT 50

title: Student Enrollments by Month
description: Tracks the number of new student enrollments per calendar month. Useful for understanding enrollment trends and planning institutional resources over time.
sql_query: SELECT DATE_FORMAT(enrollment_date, '%Y-%m') AS enrollment_month, COUNT(*) AS new_enrollments FROM students GROUP BY enrollment_month ORDER BY enrollment_month ASC LIMIT 50

title: Student Gender Distribution
description: Shows the proportion of students by gender across the entire student population. Provides a quick demographic overview for reporting and equity monitoring.
sql_query: SELECT gender AS student_gender, COUNT(*) AS student_count FROM students GROUP BY gender ORDER BY student_count DESC

title: Top Performing Students by Average Score
description: Lists the top 50 students ranked by their average percentage score across all graded subjects. Useful for recognising academic achievers and identifying high performers.
sql_query: SELECT CONCAT(st.first_name, ' ', st.last_name) AS student_name, ROUND(AVG(g.score / g.max_score * 100), 2) AS avg_score_percent FROM grades g JOIN students st ON g.student_id = st.id GROUP BY st.id, st.first_name, st.last_name ORDER BY avg_score_percent DESC LIMIT 50

title: Average Lessons Completed vs Total Lessons by Subject
description: Compares the average number of lessons students have completed against the total available lessons for each subject. Identifies subjects where students tend to drop off before finishing.
sql_query: SELECT s.name AS subject_name, s.total_lessons AS total_lessons, ROUND(AVG(ssc.lessons_completed), 2) AS avg_lessons_completed FROM student_subject_completion ssc JOIN subjects s ON ssc.subject_id = s.id GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.total_lessons ORDER BY avg_lessons_completed DESC LIMIT 50
